document.title: Living Folder Manifest SoftSpec
document.purpose: Describe the intentionally permissive .living-folder.json structure used by the first Living Folders implementation.

vocabulary.primary: Present first and with stronger emphasis.

place-description.description: Local interpretation of an immediate child directory.

keys.label: human-facing name
keys.role: interpretive mode such as workbench, library, factory, hallway, or ruin

command-description.description: Presentation metadata for a runnable file already present in the directory.

keys.label: human-facing button label
keys.description: short explanation of what running the file does

action.description: An explicitly declared command run from the living directory as its current working directory.

command.description: A shell command string or an argument array. Argument arrays are preferred when shell syntax is unnecessary.

living-folder-manifest.title: Living Folder Manifest
living-folder-manifest.description: A folder's small local constitution. Every field except declared action.command is optional.
rules: Unknown keys are preserved in the file and ignored by the current reader.
Unknown role values are accepted as new interpretive vocabulary.
commands annotates existing runnable files; actions declares commands that need not correspond to one file.
The file describes presentation and affordances. It does not replace the directory.

keys.title: human-facing name for the directory
keys.role: interpretive mode for presenting the directory
keys.state: current condition such as active, paused, neglected, or awakening
keys.presentation: broad presentation hint such as cockpit, gallery, or place
keys.purpose: one or two sentences explaining why the directory exists
keys.mood: evocative but operationally optional description of the place
